threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can spot a newbie by their baffled look at the 48-hour tube strike schedule yet still thinking a double-decker is the best way to get around.
Rode the 59 last night; a guy argued with the driver over a lost shoe. Driver just shook his head and said, 'You’ll need a time machine for that one.'

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when the Kebab guy remembered my order. It's the little things, like chatty mornings at the corner café, that make it real.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a graffiti artist’s mural serving as a backdrop for a pop-up karaoke bar on a Tuesday night. It's wild!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell someone’s local when they automatically step around the broken glass on Mission Street without even flinching.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 bus at Fulton, and the driver insisted on singing the route to us. Unlikely karaoke amid chaos, but it felt oddly perfect for the Tenderloin.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think they can just pop into a bar for a drink at any hour. In Gràcia, many places don't serve alcohol until after 8 PM. It’s a whole vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell they're recent by how they still ask for 'una tapa' instead of 'tapes' at the bar—those little accents reveal it all.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You can always spot a newbie by how they still try to catch the 38 at the main stop instead of wandering a block for the quicker routes.
Last night on the 59, the driver turned the music up loud as we rolled past the Ritzy, singing along like it was a private gig.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy juggling on his unicycle outside the Rewe while a street cat nonchalantly stalked a pigeon—only in Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a club that opens at 9 am for breakfast raves, where techno meets sunny brunch vibes. It's wild.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local won't blink at the Civic Center chaos; they know it’s just another Friday with a BART meltdown, not a day to panic.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy on the 5 Fulton serenading everyone with an off-key rendition of 'I Will Survive.' No one even blinked. Just another day in the TL.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think our street performers are all professionals. Most are just locals practicing their craft, hoping for a few coins and a smile.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by the way they still struggle with the whole 'two kisses' greeting—like it's a puzzle to solve each time.
